prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the third quarter of this year of our Lord 2022, the alchemists of the SaaS kingdom didst conjure vast sums, their coin chests swelling by a magnitude they termed "300% year-over-year growth," a figure mine own eyes could scarcely comprehend. Behold, their scribes boasted of a "churn rate" akin to a plague's mercy, yet smiled as if this were a blessing from the heavens. Lo and behold, the sorcerers of this strange trade spoke of "scalability" as if their parchments could stretch like the skin of an enchanted lizard. In their feasts, they celebrated "upgrades" and "integrations," though I know not the meaning of such strange and wondrous terms.
